mission.title: Install and Configure Kong Gateway
mission.description: Kong Gateway is a cloud-native, scalable API gateway that provides high performance and extensibility through plugins. It is ideal for managing microservices and API traffic, offering features like authentication, load balancing, and advanced AI traffic capabilities. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for ongoing management of the gateway.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the Kong CLI to manage your Kong Gateway installation.

```bash

brew install konghq/tap/kong

curl -LO https://github.com/Kong/kong/releases/download/3.9.1/kong-3.9.1-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf kong-3.9.1-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv kong /usr/local/bin/

```

> For macOS users.
> For Linux users.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the binary to your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Create a Kubernetes cluster using gcloud with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kong-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust the region as necessary.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your kubeconfig to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kong-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Install Kong Gateway
steps.description:
Deploy Kong Gateway using Helm with the specified version.

```bash

helm repo add kong https://charts.konghq.com

helm repo update

helm install kong kong/kong --version 3.9.1 --namespace kong --create-namespace

```

> Add the Kong Helm repository.
> Update your Helm repositories.
> Install Kong in the 'kong' namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for your Kong Gateway deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment kong-kong --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10 --namespace kong

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Kong/kong/master/kubernetes/monitoring/kong-monitoring.yaml

```

> Set up autoscaling for Kong.
> Deploy monitoring for Kong.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kong Gateway
uninstall.description:
This will remove the Kong Gateway and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

helm uninstall kong --namespace kong

gcloud container clusters delete kong-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Uninstall the Kong release.
> Delete the Kubernetes cluster.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kong Gateway
upgrade.description:
Ensure you backup your configuration, drain the nodes, upgrade the installation, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kong -o name | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade kong kong/kong --version 3.9.1 --namespace kong

kubectl get pods -n kong

```

> Drain the nodes before upgrade.
> Upgrade to the new version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n kong -o name | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kong Gateway is not reachable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kong Gateway pods may not be running or are in a crash loop.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl get pods -n kong' to check pod status and 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kong' for logs.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to database'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Database service may not be running or misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the database service status with 'kubectl get svc -n kong' and ensure it is accessible.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for Kong.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster or adjust resource requests/limits in the Kong deployment.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kong ingress not working'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Ingress resource may not be configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Verify your ingress resource with 'kubectl describe ingress <ingress-name> -n kong'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new authentication plugins and improved performance for existing plugins.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 3.8, ensure to update your authentication configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get pods -n kong' showing all pods in 'Running' status.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
